sourceEvidence: KOC 2012 EXAM (2).pdf, page 26 (rendered image, not text layer). The paper prints verbatim: "Q111: What are two purposes of segmenting a network with a bridge?" / "A. To reduce collisions" (bold + underlined = the paper's own key marking) / "B. To increase collisions" / "C. To reduce collision domains" / "D. To have more broadcast domains". There is NO "(Choose two.)" marker anywhere on the page, only four options, and only option A is bolded/underlined.
note: Triage's two hypotheses are both refuted: the paper never printed a choose-two marker, and it prints option C as "To reduce collision domains" exactly as we shipped. Our extraction is byte-faithful; the flaw (a 'two purposes' stem with a single keyed answer, i.e. a corrupted rendition of the CCNA original) is in the source paper itself, so per the fidelity rule it stays verbatim. Item remains answerable (A is uniquely correct and is the paper's own key).

sourceEvidence: Test 3.zip, IMG_9099.HEIC (page 2), question 5. Zoomed crop of the option block shows: "a. 100n log n = O(n log n / 100)" / "b. sqrt(log n) = O(log log n)" (circled in red = key) / "c. If 0 < x < y then n^x = O(n^y)" -- x and y are unambiguously raised superscripts -- / "d. 2^n != O(nk)" -- the n carries a clear raised superscript but the k sits on the BASELINE, same size and italic weight as the n beside it, i.e. the paper literally prints O(nk), not O(n^k).
note: Source typo, not an extraction bug: the paper itself dropped the superscript on k. Per the fidelity rule the typo is kept verbatim. The item still answers cleanly -- with O(nk) read literally, statement d is still true (2^n is not O(nk)), so B remains the unique false statement and the key is unaffected.

sourceEvidence: Test 3.zip, IMG_9100.HEIC (page 3), question 10. Zoomed crop shows: "10. Maximum number of nodes in a binary tree with height k, where root is height 0, is" / "A. 2^k - 1" / "B. 2^(k+1) - 1" (circled in red = key) / "C. 2^(k-1) + 1" / "D. 2^k - 1". Options A and D are printed identically in the paper: both read 2^k - 1, superscript k, minus one.
note: BROKEN AT SOURCE. The duplicate option is the paper's own defect, not ours -- our extraction reproduced it faithfully. Nothing to restore (there is no 'real D' to recover), so per instructions it is quarantined rather than repaired. Note the key B is correct and the paper circles it; only the duplicate distractor is broken.

sourceEvidence: Test 3.zip, IMG_9101.HEIC (page 4), question 19. Zoomed crop of the stem line shows exactly: "19. Time complexity of Depth First Traversal of is" -- no word, no underscore, and no whitespace gap between "of" and "is". For contrast, question 14 on the same page prints a real blank that renders clearly as "From a complete graph, by removing maximum ________ edges, we can construct a spanning tree.", so blanks on this page are visible when present. Options: "A. Θ(|V| + |E|)" (circled = key) / "B. Θ(|V|)" / "C. Θ(|E|)" / "D. Θ(|V| * |E|)".
note: Source typo, not an extraction bug: the paper itself omitted the words after "of" (no blank was printed there either). Per the fidelity rule the stem is kept verbatim. Item is answerable and the key A matches the paper's circle.

sourceEvidence: Test 3.zip, IMG_9102.HEIC (page 5), question 22. Zoomed crop shows: "22. All possible spanning trees of graph G" -- bare fragment, no colon, no ellipsis, no blank -- then "A. Have same number of edges and vertices." (circled in red = key) / "B. Have same number of edges and but not vertices." / "C. Have same number of vertices but not edges." / "D. Depends upon algorithm being used." The doubled conjunction "and but" is printed in the paper.
note: Both triage concerns resolve to source fidelity: the paper itself prints the "and but" word salad in option B, and it prints the stem as a bare fragment with no completion marker. Per the fidelity rule both are kept verbatim. Item is answerable and the key A matches the paper's circle.
